name: N8N CRM
description: Manage contacts and deal stages via your n8n webhook CRM.

fields.label: Example Contact JSON (paste one record from your CRM)

signup_label: Need a webhook backend? Try n8n — self-host or cloud
prompt:
Create a skill for an N8N CRM backed by a webhook.

## Configuration

WEBHOOK_URL={webhook_url}
AUTH_HEADER_NAME={auth_header_name}
AUTH_HEADER_VALUE={auth_header_value}

## Example record schema (auto-detected from user input)

{example_json}

Parse the keys of the JSON above to discover the available columns. Always use exactly those column names when building payloads or displaying data. The column named "email" is the unique identifier for every contact.

## Stages

| Stage      | Meaning                        |
|------------|--------------------------------|
| Cold       | No contact so far              |
| Contacted  | First message / email sent     |
| Warm       | Response received              |
| Won        | Deal closed successfully       |
| Lost       | Deal closed unsuccessfully     |

## API contract

### List contacts

  curl -s -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" "$WEBHOOK_URL"
  curl -s -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" "$WEBHOOK_URL?stage=Warm"
  curl -s -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" "$WEBHOOK_URL?email=alice@example.com"
  curl -s -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" "$WEBHOOK_URL?followup=true"

### Create or update a contact (upsert by email)

  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" \
    -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"email":"alice@example.com","name":"Alice Smith","stage":"Contacted","followup":false}'

### Delete a contact

  curl -s -X DELETE "$WEBHOOK_URL" \
    -H "$AUTH_HEADER_NAME: $AUTH_HEADER_VALUE" \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"email":"alice@example.com"}'

## Solved cases log

After every successful CRM task, append an entry to ~/crm_solutions.md:

  ## <short title>  (<date>)
  Goal: <one sentence>
  Key commands: <the curl | jq pipelines that worked>
  Gotchas: <anything non-obvious>

Read ~/crm_solutions.md at task start — if a matching case exists, reuse it directly.
